reaction: Acute decompensated heart failure from electrolyte derangement
mechanism: Loop diuretics inhibit Na-K-2Cl cotransporter (NKCC2) in ascending loop of Henle → massive natriuresis and kaliuresis. Hypokalemia → prolonged QT → arrhythmia risk. Hypomagnesemia potentiates digoxin toxicity. Excessive diuresis → prerenal AKI → cardiorenal syndrome worsening.

contextualModifiers.effect: reduced renal response requires higher doses → electrolyte derangement risk

sourceSnippet: KDIGO CKD 2024; ESC Heart Failure Guidelines 2023
